head.hi: The Secretary of State to certain American Consuls in Mexico.

opener.seg: [Telegram.]Sent to Aguascalientes, Guadalajara, Hermosillo, Tapachula, San Luis Potosi, Tuxpam, Mazatlan, and Guaymas, adding to the last three, “Naval vessel instructed to afford transportation.”

opener.dateline:
Department of State,
Washington, April 24, 1914, 9 a.m.

div.p: In view of relations existing between this Government and the Huerta Government, you and such members of your staff as are American citizens are directed to leave your post at the earliest opportunity. Place consular offices and archives with coats of arms, flags and seals in custody of the consular officer of France and proceed to the United States or place of safety, bringing code, current accounts and fee stamps with you. Procure vouchers for transportation expenses of your family and instruct members of staff to do likewise.

opener.seg: [Telegram.]Sent to Puerto Mexico and Progreso, adding to the latter the word “code” after “bringing,” and omitting last sentence.

frus:attachment.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline:
Department of State,
Washington, April 24, 1914, 11 a.m.

frus:attachment.p: In view of relations existing [etc. as in above telegram] place of safety, bringing current accounts and fee stamps with you. Procure vouchers for transportation expenses of your family.

opener.seg: [Telegram.]Sent to Puebla and Oaxaca; same to Guanajuato, adding “code” after “bringing.”

frus:attachment.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline:
Department of State,
Washington, April 24, 1914, 11 a.m.

frus:attachment.p: In view of relations existing between this Government and the Huerta Government, you are directed [etc. as in next above].

opener.seg: [Telegram.]Sent to Chihuahua; some to Saltillo, Monterey and Tampieo, omitting last sentence; same to Durango, substituting Tapia and Torreon for Parral. Same to Hermosillo down to “general situation”; then read: “This telegram supersedes that of April 24, 9 a.m. Agent Guaymas has been appropriately instructed direct.”

frus:attachment.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline:
Department of State,
Washington, April 25, 1914, 2 p.m.

frus:attachment.p: In view of uncertainty in situation in Northern Mexico, you and such members of your staff as are American citizens are authorized to leave your post using your discretion as to time of departure. When you decide to leave, place consular office and archives, with coats of arms, flags and seals in the custody of the French consular officer and take with you the code, current accounts and fee stamps. Procure vouchers for transportation expenses of your family and instruct members of your staff to do likewise. Will keep you advised as far as possible of general situation. Endeavor inform Parral directing Agent to close office and securely store archives, coats of arms, flags and seals.

opener.seg: [Telegram.]Sent to Matamoros, Juárez, Díaz and Nuevo Laredo; same to Nogales, adding: “Endeavor to communicate foregoing to Cananea, directing Agent to close office and securely store archives, coats of arms, flags and seals.”

frus:attachment.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline:
Department of State,
Washington, April 24, 1914, 2 p.m.

frus:attachment.p: In view [etc. as in next above] departure. When you decide to leave, close the consular office and transfer archives with coats of arms, flags and seals to safe place in United States, taking with you the code, current accounts and fee stamps. Procure vouchers for transportation expenses of your family and instruct members of your staff to do likewise. Will keep you advised as far as possible of general situation.

frus:attachment.head: [Untitled]

opener.dateline:
Department of State,
Washington, April 25, 1914, 9 p.m.

frus:attachment.p: In view of relations existing between this Government and the Huerta Government, you and such members of your staff as are American citizens are directed to leave your post at the earliest opportunity. Place consular offices and archives with coats of arms, flags and seals in custody of the consular officer of Great Britain and proceed to the United States or place of safety bringing code, current accounts and fee stamps with you. Procure vouchers for transportation expenses of your family. Naval vessel instructed to afford transportation.
